terminology.note: D&D Beyond changed labels on 2026-03-02. This was a terminology change only; internal values remain stable for saved-data compatibility.

statusDefinitions.verified: Checked against the cited primary source and protected by a test or exact-data comparison.
statusDefinitions.verified-with-heuristic: Official data is verified, but a clearly labeled DM Forge heuristic is also present.
statusDefinitions.homebrew: Original or user-created content; it must never be represented as official rules.
statusDefinitions.names-only: The interface lists a rule name but intentionally directs the DM to official text for adjudication.
statusDefinitions.in-progress: Source review is underway and the record must not be advertised as fully verified.
statusDefinitions.needs-correction: A known omission or mismatch exists and should be fixed before production claims are made.

notes: Official 5e thresholds and multiple-monster multipliers are exact.
Official 5.5e Low, Moderate, and High XP budgets are exact.
CR 0 explicitly supports 0 or 10 XP; explicit zero values are preserved.
Mixed-party average-level cautions and CR-spread review prompts are labeled DM Forge heuristics.
5.5e encounters above the High budget are now labeled Above High.
The full-caster slot progression used by the artifact is shared by the supported 5e and 5.5e Cleric tables.
Only leveled Cleric spells are included because the artifact exposes level 1-9 charges, not cantrips.
Potion formulas are the standard 2d4+2, 4d4+4, 8d4+8, and 10d4+20 progression. The artifact's free-action use is homebrew and documented separately.

Base formulas for Cure Wounds, Healing Word, Guiding Bolt, Inflict Wounds, Aid, Prayer of Healing, Spiritual Weapon, Glyph of Warding, Mass Healing Word, Revivify, Spirit Guardians, Guardian of Faith, Insect Plague, Mass Cure Wounds, Blade Barrier, Harm, Heal, Regenerate, and Mass Heal have been checked in the current pass.
Flame Strike has a known edition difference: 8d6 total in 5e and 10d6 total in 5.5e; the current data model requires correction.
Spiritual Weapon and Spirit Guardians require edition-specific concentration and trigger notes.
Every remaining resurrection, radiant, and area-damage summary must be checked before this component becomes verified.
Free-action activation, immunity to theft/destruction/suppression, and deity-only control are campaign artifact rules, not core D&D rules.
Goblin 5e sample is substantially complete for its displayed fields.
Adult Black Dragon and Lich samples omit encounter-critical DCs, damage values, slot counts, and complete action text.
The generic source label SRD sample must be replaced by exact SRD version and license metadata.
These cards must not be described as complete production stat blocks until corrected.
The board intentionally lists names and prompts only and tells the DM to use the selected ruleset's official text for exact adjudication.
The builder stores user-created rules. Presets must identify whether they are original DM Forge content, user homebrew, or licensed rules content.
Generator tables are original prompts and must not be labeled official treasure, NPC, or encounter tables.
